Part 2: Description of Design Change 
Summarize the proposed design change 
Part 3: : Documents that define the previously approved configuration 
List the approval certificate and all documents which define the approved configuration upon which this change is will be applied (E.g. MDL, 
ICA, etc…) 
Certificate / Document Number Revision Title / Titre 
example: A-LSA15-123 xx example: Design Compliance Report 
example: MDL-XXXX             
example: DCR-XXXX             
                  
Part 4: Certification Plan 
The following documents define the Certification Basis, the Means and Methods of Compliance, the requirements where TC is responsible for 
the finding of compliance, the Level of Involvement and the Level of Service that was agreed upon for the previous approvals of this 
modification. 
(See Appendix A for CPR determination for this change) 
Number Revision Title Comments 
(E.g. if accepted with comments, etc…) 
COM-XXXX  xx Certification Plan - example:  Galley 
Installation  
COM-XXXX-1  xx Certification Plan - example:  Galley 
Installation  
Part 5: Evaluation Checklist  
Question Yes No 
1. Does the proposed change fall within the scope of the original certification plan, or is the change small 
in nature? 
(E.g. addition of or revision to a drawing, document or deliverable; moving or changing a feature, installation or monument; addition 
of an aircraft serial number; addition of a similar configuration; change to a part number; etc…) 
  
2. Has the CPR evaluation included herein (See Appendix A) been evaluated against the cumulative 
effect of this and past changes?   
3. Does the basis of certification remain valid for the proposed change?   
4. Are the paragraphs applicable to this design change a subset of the existing cert basis? 
If NO: Identify any new regulations in part 6 and check the box “Added to certification basis”   
5. Do the means and methods of compliance agreed upon via the certification plan of Part 4 remain 
unchanged? 
If NO: Define the affected paragraph(s) and the new or changed MOC in the comments section of part 6. 
  
6. Do the substantiating documents / deliverables defined via the documents in Part 3 remain unchanged? 
If NO: Define the new document(s) or the document(s) requiring revision and, the proposed changes to these documents in Part 7.   
7. Do all of the statements, limitations and documents referenced on, or approved via the approval 
certificate remain valid? 
If NO, Describe the required changes to the certificate and justification in part 7

title: PDF Page 3
content:
Appendix A to COM-XXXX Rev A 
 
  
CHANGED PRODUCT RULE (CPR) DECISION RECORD 
NAPA No.: A-17-0XXX 
Step 1: Identify the proposed change 
to the aeronautical product. 
(Section 4.1 of AC 500-016) 
The changes are as follows or are detailed in the listed document(s): 
Water Door Caution System, as documented in the Certification Plan COM-XXXX 
Note:    A separate Decision Record or G-1 Issue Paper must be raised to address the decisions at Step 2 and Steps 4 through 6, and the 
certification basis. 
Step 2: Is the change substantial? 
(Section 4.2 of AC 500-016) 
 Yes 
 No 
A new type certificate is required. CPR Decision Process is Closed. 
Proceed to Step 3 
Step 3: Will the latest standards be 
used? 
(Section 4.3 of AC 500-016) 
 Yes 
 No 
Certification basis to use latest standards. CPR Decision Process is Closed.  
Proceed to Step 4. 
Step 4: Is the proposed change 
significant? 
(Section 4.4 of AC 500-016) 
 Yes 
 No 
Proceed to Decision. 
Compliance may be shown to earlier standards. Certification basis to be defined 
and documented as indicated (below). CPR Decision Process is Closed. 
Decision: Will the latest standards be 
used? 
 Yes 
 No 
Certification basis to use latest standards. CPR Decision Process is Closed. 
Proceed to Step 5, addressing each area separately (see below). 
Identification of Affected Areas: The area(s) affected by the proposed change have been detailed in Certification Plan or Compliance 
Program (or equivalent) document number(s): 
 
Note:    A delegate may develop a proposal for the Yes/No decision of Step 6, however, TCCA will make the final determination. 
Area:  
Step 5: Is this area affected by the 
proposed change? 
(Section 6.1 of AC 500-016) 
 Yes 
 No 
Proceed to Step 6. 
Compliance with the latest standards is not required. Compliance may be shown 
to earlier standards. Certification basis defined or documented as indicated 
below. 
Step 6:  Are the latest standards 
practical and do they contribute 
materially to the level of safety? 
(Section 6.2 of AC 500-016) 
 Yes 
 No 
Certification basis to be established using latest standards. 
Compliance with the latest standards is not required. Compliance may be shown 
to earlier standards. Certification Basis defined or documented as indicated in 
below. 
Note:  Several standards may apply to each area and the assessment may 
differ from standard to standard. Indicate Yes if compliance with any latest 
standard(s) will be required.  Indicate No only if no later standards are to 
be applied.  
 Continuation Sheet(s) Attached 
Certification Basis  
 
 
The certification basis is as follows or as detailed in the listed document(s): 
 
As the proposed change is not significant following the guidance of AC 500-016 (Table 
A.3.3, Item 8), the certification basis shall be as presented in the Certification Plan COM-
XXXX Rev A accepted by Transport Canada. 
Under the delegated authority, I have examined the change in type design listed above according to established procedures and 
hereby determine, to the best of my knowledge and belief, that it is. (check one) 
  substantial, pursuant to subsection 511.14 or 513.14 of the CARs 
  significant, pursuant to subsection 511.13(3) or 513.07(3) of the CARs 
  not significant, pursuant to subsection 511.13(3) or 513.07(3) of the CARs
